title: Stand up a fresh context substrate for the port via curated carry-forward
context: The operation-framework port needs a substrate baseline; the active .context is the pi-context dev substrate and not a clean one. Substrate switching is non-destructive — the active pointer flips like a git branch and the parked substrate stays on disk (as .project / .context-jit-spec-v2 / .project-archived already coexist).
decision: Rename the pi-context dev substrate from .context to .context-archived-2026-06-30, freeing the canonical .context name. Stand up a new substrate at .context for the operation-framework port. Curated carry-forward of verified-live items only — not blind greenfield, not wholesale copy. The archived substrate stays on disk, queryable, reactivable via context-switch.
consequences: The port's PM state lives at the canonical .context — no naming indirection; all conventions/defaults/tooling land on it.
The pi-context dev substrate is preserved at .context-archived-2026-06-30 (parked, queryable, not lost).
The port's feature/task decomposition (PHASE-PORT-OPS) is filed on the new .context, not the archived one.
A curation triage is required: enumerate live items from the archived substrate and re-file only verified-good keepers.
The archived substrate's config.json root and .pi-context.json pointer require fixup before reactivation (one-time, on-demand).
